## 2. 2020–2021 Campus and Facility Safety Audits

**Presenter:** Michael Ferguson, School Safety Specialist

**Background on Ferguson:** 23 years as an athletic trainer; opened College Park High School in 2005; served as assistant principal at Knox Junior High for four years; moved into safety specialist role in January 2020 (approximately 1.5 years in position).

**Audit Overview:**
- The district completed its first three-year rotation of campus safety audits in 2019–2020.
- A new rotation began in 2020–2021 covering **district facilities, Conroe High School feeder zone, and Caney Creek feeder zone**.
- Audits were delayed to the spring semester to allow campuses to settle into the unusual pandemic school year.

**Audit Process:**
- Safety audit team typically includes Ferguson plus an assistant/associate principal; sometimes Ethan Martin or the campus principal.
- Involves walkthroughs, teacher Q&A sessions about safety concerns and the Multi-Hazard Emergency Operating Procedure (MEOP), and reviewing evacuation routes.
- **Intruder assessments** are conducted: Ferguson attempts to gain access to campuses unannounced, noting whether and how quickly staff identify and stop an unrecognized person. Staff who properly challenge him receive positive recognition.
- Findings are reported to campus leadership, with corrections made on-site when possible.

**Most Common Findings:**
1. **Mechanical rooms** — Items stored improperly blocking access to equipment
2. **Storage stacked too high** — Fire code requires 18 inches clearance from sprinklers; district standard is 24 inches for consistency (not all campuses have automatic sprinklers)
3. **Daisy-chaining electrical cords** — Surge protectors plugged into each other
4. **Unsecured personal belongings** — Teacher purses/bags left in the open where medications or valuables could be accessed
5. **Knives and sharp instruments** — Particularly in CTE/culinary classrooms; a new check-in/check-out tracking system will be implemented next year in collaboration with the CTE department

**Key Takeaway:** The district performs well overall; most findings are easily correctable issues.

**Committee Input:** None offered at this time; members encouraged to provide input via email or phone.

---

## 3. Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) — Inventory and Replacement Schedule

**Presenters:** Chris McCord and Rick Reeves

**Current Status:**
- The district is in a significantly better position regarding COVID-19 mitigation compared to one year ago.
- **Barbara Robertson** was specifically recognized for her critical role in enabling the district to open for in-person instruction throughout the year.
- Partners acknowledged: Memorial Hermann, Montgomery County, St. Luke's, Lone Star Family Health.

**Campus-Level PPE:**
- Campuses received funds in 2020–2021 to identify and purchase building-specific PPE needs.
- **Funds are budgeted again for 2021–2022** for continued PPE needs as necessary.

**District Warehouse/Inventory:**
- Approximately **60 pallets** of hand sanitizer, masks, and gloves were received from the state in June 2020.
- An off-site warehouse was secured (with help from Sarah Blakelock) to store state-provided supplies, as the main warehouse was staging Suchma Elementary opening supplies.
- Items distributed district-wide include: gloves, wipes (for Chromebooks/desks), sneeze guards/barriers, and hand sanitizer stands with refills.
- **Remaining inventory is substantial** — still includes pallets of hand sanitizer, face shields, thermometers, gloves, masks, KN95s, and more.
- Donations continue to arrive (Home Depot donated two pallets of face masks; Sam's Club also donated). The district has begun **declining additional hand sanitizer donations** and referring donors to surrounding districts.

**Purchasing:**
- In October 2020, the district **awarded a PPE bid to 120 vendors** — one of few districts to do so — enabling rapid procurement (7–10 day turnaround) when co-op vendors ran low.
- Campuses also have access to the vetted vendor list for direct legal purchases using allocated funds.

**Key Statements:**
- **McCord:** "We moved heaven and earth to get PPE when you couldn't find PPE because we were going to protect our staff so that we could open."
- McCord noted pride that CISD opened months before many other districts were announcing readiness to open.

**Committee Input:** No additional input; members encouraged to reach out anytime.

---

## 4. Planning and Construction — Summer Campus Safety and Security Upgrades

**Presenter:** Easy Foster, Director of Planning and Construction

**Overview:** Presented the status of projects from the **2019 bond referendum** plan. 2020 projects are complete; 2021 projects are underway.

**Campuses currently under work (2021):**
- Ford, Kaufman, Oak Ridge Elementary, Oak Ridge Warehouse Transportation, Vogel, Haley, The Woodlands Transportation, and Testing & Assessment

**Standard Safety/Security Upgrades included in all projects (renovations, new construction, and standalone security upgrades):**

| Upgrade | Description |
|---|---|
| **Secure vestibules** | New or improved secure entry points |
| **Impact-resistant window film** | Applied to all windows surrounding reception areas |
| **Electronic access control** | Upgraded for all exterior doors |
| **Entry/exit alarm systems** | (Elementary, intermediate, junior high) Doors monitored from reception with local hallway alarms for unauthorized entry/exit; real-time visual monitoring |
| **First responder radio antenna systems** | Full 100% radio coverage for fire and police inside all buildings; to be completed district-wide by end of bond plan |
| **District digital radio antenna systems** | Eliminating dead spots for local campus radio communication |
| **Security camera and server upgrades** | Cameras linked to access control for automatic activation on unauthorized entry/exit; blind spots being identified and eliminated |
| **Security system (burglar panel) upgrades** | Updated for full building coverage |
| **Fire alarm system upgrades** | Transition from horn/strobe to **voice evacuation systems** with visual strobes and calming voice guidance toward exits (fire code requirement) |

**Key Note:** By the completion of the 2019 bond plan, **all educational buildings** in the district will have first responder radio systems and the full suite of security upgrades.

**Committee Input:** None at this time.

## 5. Approval of Multi-Hazard Emergency Operations Plan for 2021–2022

**Presenter:** Ethan Martin

**Changes from prior year's plan:**
- Updated names and dates (committee membership changes)
- Added **tornado terminology** (watch vs. warning clarification) for campus reference
- Minor/minimal changes overall
- A typo in the agenda was noted (listed as one year instead of "2021–2022")

**Committee members had been asked to provide input/feedback on the plan following the March 30 meeting; Martin thanked those who responded.**

**Vote:**
- **Motion to approve:** *(member not clearly identified)*
- **Second:** Mr. Moore
- **Discussion:** None
- **Vote:** Approved unanimously by show of hands (no opposition voiced)
- The plan will now proceed to the full school board for consideration.

---

## Other Business / Closing Remarks

- **Ethan Martin** reminded members that the committee is required to meet **three times per year**; this was the summer meeting. The next meeting will be in the fall.
- Members were asked to **email topics or issues** they would like the committee to address before the next meeting.
- **Dr. Knoll** thanked all attendees, including those joining during summer vacation, and wished everyone a safe summer.
